title: Campium palustre
taxonomy: Campium palustre is a type of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. It is classified within the genus Campium and is known to be native to certain regions. This plant is further classified as a member of the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants. At the phylum level, Campium palustre is classified as a member of the phylum Pteridophyta. It is also known to be a part of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Campium palustre has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. It has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate various health issues. The plant is believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating infections. In traditional medicine, Campium palustre has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Campium palustre as a medicinal plant.

parts: Campium palustre, also known as marsh bellflower, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine.
